audits.scope: Avantis core perpetuals smart contracts, ZFP system, liquidity provider vault mechanics
audits.summary: In-depth security audit by industry-leading firm Zellic (trusted by LayerZero, Aptos, Pyth) prior to mainnet deployment on Base. Zellic audit verified code quality and identified issues before mainnet launch.

audits.scope: Comprehensive security audit of Avantis protocol contracts and risk framework
audits.summary: Comprehensive audit by Zokyo (trusted by SushiSwap, Solana Foundation, 1inch) covering full protocol codebase. Zokyo delivered security verification of Avantis' live contracts prior to mainnet operations.

code_health.upgrade_mechanism: proxy (UUPS-style or standard proxy pattern; specific admin/timelock details not publicly documented)
code_health.bug_bounty_platform: Not identified on public Immunefi directory

dependencies.notes: Avantis operates on Base mainnet; sequencer downtime or censorship would prevent trade execution and settlement

Perpetuals DEX heavily dependent on ultra-low-latency price feeds from Pyth for mark price and liquidation logic. Avantis appears Pyth-affiliated. Oracle downtime or stale prices directly trigger liquidations and trade execution failures.
Secondary oracle provider for price feed redundancy. Avantis compares Pyth vs Chainlink prices; >5% deviation auto-rejects trades. Both oracles critical for risk management.
USDC is settlement and collateral asset. Vault liquidity pool backed entirely by USDC deposits; USDC depegging or freeze would impact LP withdrawals and position unwinding.

Protocol design creates LP-vs-trader conflict: LPs earn trader losses but face open PnL deficits during prolonged adverse price moves. Buffer depletion < 0.95 activates AVNT staking slashing module; max 20% slashing penalty on stakers. New protocol with unproven extreme-scenario behavior.

rationale: Avantis is a YOUNG protocol (mainnet Feb 2024, AVNT token Sept 2025) with STRONG audit coverage (Zellic + Zokyo pre-launch). NO EXPLOIT HISTORY to date. However, age and complexity warrant elevated caution: (1) Dual-oracle dependency (Pyth + Chainlink) introduces high counterparty risk; Pyth stale prices or Chainlink circuit breakers could trigger cascade liquidations; (2) LP vault design with AVNT slashing creates novel game-theoretic risk during vault buffer depletion—untested under extreme volatility (2024 launch avoided crypto crash scenarios); (3) Base L2 sequencer dependency (medium risk, but Coinbase-operated sequencer more centralized than Ethereum L1); (4) No visible bug bounty program (missing Immunefi or equivalent), reducing post-launch crowdsourced security; (5) Proxy upgrade mechanism not publicly documented—usual practice is timelock+multisig but not confirmed. Suitable for informed traders with perp leverage experience; NOT suitable for risk-averse LP capital. Protocol treasury covers losses before slashing (positive), but slashing mechanics untested.
